license.notice: Event descriptions are paraphrased summaries written for this dataset. Source links point to authoritative references.

entries.title: Task registered
entries.summary: A new scheduled task was registered.
entries.details: Generated when a new scheduled task is registered in the Task Scheduler. Key field: TaskName (full path, e.g., \Microsoft\Windows\TaskName). Complements Security EID 4698 but fires independently and may capture task registrations that do not generate a Security audit event depending on audit policy configuration. Tasks registered under the root folder (\TaskName with no subfolder) rather than a vendor-specific subfolder are more commonly associated with manual or malicious creation.

investigationPivots: Tasks in the root scheduler namespace (no subfolder) warrant closer inspection
Correlate TaskName with Security EID 4698 TaskContent XML to review the full task definition
Check the registering user context — SYSTEM-registered tasks created outside of normal software installs are suspicious
commonFalsePositives: Software installers and updaters registering scheduled tasks

entries.title: Task updated
entries.summary: An existing scheduled task was updated.
entries.details: Generated when the definition of an existing scheduled task is modified. Key field: TaskName. Does not include the new task XML content in this log — correlate with Security EID 4702 (TaskContentNew field) to review what changed. Modifications to tasks outside of known software update cycles, especially changes to the action executable or trigger conditions, are a persistence modification indicator.

investigationPivots: Correlate with Security EID 4702 to review the updated task XML
Compare timing of the update against known software update windows
commonFalsePositives: Software auto-updaters modifying their own scheduled tasks
Group Policy refreshes updating task definitions

entries.title: Task removed
entries.details: Generated when a scheduled task is removed from the Task Scheduler. Key field: TaskName. Deletion of a recently created or recently triggered task may indicate cleanup of a malicious persistence mechanism. Correlate TaskName with EID 106 and EID 200 to determine whether the task was ever executed before deletion.

investigationPivots: Correlate with EID 106 to determine when the task was originally created
Correlate with EID 200 to confirm whether the task executed before being deleted
Deletion shortly after execution is a strong cleanup indicator
commonFalsePositives: Software uninstallers removing their own scheduled tasks
One-time setup tasks that self-delete after running

entries.title: Action started
entries.summary: A scheduled task action (executable) began executing.
entries.details: Generated when a scheduled task action starts. Key fields: TaskName and ActionName (the executable or script path that was launched). This is the primary execution evidence event in the Task Scheduler log and is not captured by the Security log EIDs 4698-4702, which only cover task lifecycle (create/modify/delete/enable), not execution. Pivot on ActionName to identify what was run and correlate with Security EID 4688 to find the resulting process creation event.

investigationPivots: ActionName is the key field — inspect the full executable path and any arguments
Correlate TaskName with EID 106 to determine how long the task existed before execution
Cross-reference ActionName with Security EID 4688 CommandLine for full argument visibility (if command-line auditing is enabled)
Execution from temp directories, user profile paths, or with encoded/obfuscated arguments is a strong malicious indicator
commonFalsePositives: Legitimate software running scheduled maintenance or update tasks
Windows built-in tasks (e.g., Defrag, Windows Defender scans)

entries.title: Action completed
entries.summary: A scheduled task action finished executing.
entries.details: Generated when a scheduled task action finishes. Key fields: TaskName, ActionName, and ResultCode. A ResultCode of 0 indicates successful completion. Non-zero result codes may indicate the action failed or was terminated. Pair with EID 200 using TaskName to measure execution duration. Abnormally short execution times for tasks that should run longer, or consistent non-zero result codes, may indicate detection and termination by a security tool.

investigationPivots: Pair with EID 200 using TaskName to calculate execution duration
Non-zero ResultCode may indicate the process was killed or failed — correlate with Defender or EDR telemetry
Very short durations (sub-second) combined with non-zero result codes may indicate security tool intervention
commonFalsePositives: Any legitimate scheduled task completing normally
